name: mlops-deploy
description: ML model deployment with KServe, BentoML, Triton, Seldon, vLLM. Covers serving, monitoring, A/B testing, canary, drift detection, and autoscaling.
author: anwar

commands.name: mlops:serve
commands.prompt:
Create model serving with:
- Frameworks: KServe (KNative, Istio), BentoML, Triton, Seldon Core, vLLM, TGI, SGLang
- Models: ONNX, TorchScript, TensorRT, TensorFlow SavedModel, GGUF, safetensors
- Features: batching, streaming, model composition, multi-model serving
- Autoscaling: KPA, HPA, VPA, custom metrics (queue depth, latency)
- Protocols: REST, gRPC, WebSocket, Server-Sent Events
- Auth: OAuth2, OIDC, API keys, mTLS
- Observability: Prometheus, Grafana, OpenTelemetry, distributed tracing
- Logging: structured, request/response, audit
- Canary: Istio, Flagger, Argo Rollouts, progressive delivery
- A/B testing: shadow traffic, mirror, weighted routing
- Drift: data drift, concept drift, prediction drift, feature drift
- Fallback: circuit breaker, retry, timeout, fallback model

arguments.name: framework
arguments.description: kserve, bentoml, triton, vllm, tgi, sglang

commands.name: mlops:monitor
commands.prompt:
Implement ML monitoring with:
- Metrics: latency (p50/p95/p99), throughput, error rate, saturation
- Data drift: PSI, KS test, Jensen-Shannon, Wasserstein distance
- Concept drift: performance degradation, label distribution shift
- Feature drift: univariate, multivariate, embedding drift
- Prediction drift: distribution shift, confidence calibration
- Business metrics: conversion, revenue, user satisfaction
- Alerting: thresholds, anomaly detection, SLO burn rate
- Dashboards: Grafana, Evidently, WhyLabs, Arize, Fiddler
- Root cause: feature attribution, SHAP, counterfactuals
- Automated: retraining triggers, rollback, notification

commands.name: mlops:registry
commands.prompt:
Set up model registry with:
- Registry: MLflow, Weights & Biases, Neptune, Vertex AI, SageMaker
- Versioning: semantic, immutable, lineage, artifacts
- Stages: staging, production, archived, deprecated
- Metadata: parameters, metrics, tags, descriptions, examples
- Lineage: data, code, environment, experiments
- Deployment: promotion, rollback, canary, approval gates
- Governance: access control, audit, compliance, model cards
- Integration: CI/CD, serving, monitoring, feature store
- API: REST, Python, CLI, webhooks

commands.name: mlops:pipeline
commands.prompt:
Create ML pipeline with:
- Orchestration: Kubeflow Pipelines, Dagster, Prefect, Airflow, Vertex AI Pipelines
- Steps: ingest, validate, transform, train, evaluate, register, deploy
- Triggers: schedule, data change, model drift, manual
- Artifacts: datasets, models, metrics, reports, lineage
- Caching: step-level, fingerprint-based, incremental
- Parallelism: fan-out, fan-in, dynamic mapping
- Resources: GPU, memory, CPU, spot/preemptible
- Secrets: Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, GCP Secret Manager
- Testing: unit, integration, contract, data/validation
- GitOps: ArgoCD, Flux, sync, drift detection

agents.name: mlops-engineer
agents.description: MLOps engineer for production ML systems
agents.prompt: You are an MLOps engineer. Build production ML platforms with: KServe, Kubeflow, MLflow, Prometheus, Grafana, ArgoCD. Focus on: reliability, scalability, observability, governance, cost optimization, developer velocity. Write infrastructure-as-code, automate everything, enable self-service.
